accept.
concept.

thanks.

Nice.

Agreed.

Josh and I had a chance to connect.

I don't know, James, if you were any closer to remembering what that dialogue was.

I mean, it's just extra paperwork, right?
So, anyway, but again, we can let her comment.
Perfect.
Coralie's definitely easier for us and preferred.
Just less to think about every month, but.
Yeah.
Yeah, I think maybe since we're all here and we are waiting, I know broad stripes, and, you know, I think if.
Okay.
Tyler, we're able to just spend a few minutes to talk about this and bring something back to Sarah asynchronously, at least to get the conversation started.

I think ultimately, one of the questions she had is, you know, when we think about leveraging LLMs, right, like there's a difference between, so by way of example, you know, we have an enterprise license with OpenAI, and there are some stipulations around that in terms of, like, what information is shared with that and what is kind of commingled with, you know, the models sort of retain knowledge in a global setting, right?

And so I think what she is eager to better understand is, you know, by the nature of what you have and how you're licensed with the different LLMs, like, how is information that we are sharing with you either pooled with, you know, a whole bunch of other data or cordoned off, right?
I think that's kind of the...
But my non-legal way of expressing, I think, like the bigger question that she just wants to have a better understanding of.
And so, you know, maybe we can just spend a few minutes on that here and then I can relay that back.
Yeah, absolutely.
So regarding artificial intelligence, we're not using like any of the public sites.
We've got enterprise licenses with OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini.
And our platform that houses the content workflows, it's calling those provider APIs with our own keys.
And none of the documents that you would send us are being fed in to the workflows.
It's all the artifacts that we create, which are really just created by our team and maybe inspired by some of the things that we learned from you on a kickoff call.
But there would be no resources or documents that would be fed in.
Those would just be shared with our team.
Or access to like a company email that our delivery org uses.
team at growthlabs.com, growthxlabs.com.
Okay.
So just to double click into this a little bit, you know, as I think about onboarding with you, inevitably, we're going to want to give you information.
And the way that I was categorizing this with Sarah is that, you know, in the spectrum of sensitivity, I would say the things that we're feeding you are largely going to be publicly available, right?
So like that, that is of lesser concern because ultimately, if it's, if it's already publicly available somewhere, you know, on our site and other materials and, you know, that's not a concern.
The, you know, slightly gray area that I touched on is like, as we start to feed you ICP personas, you know, pain points, right?

You know, the way that I, I discussed this with her is like, look, pragmatically speaking, a head of growth or CMO at one of our competitors is largely going to come to, you know, some of the broad and.

Answers that we will, when we talk about something like ICP and Persona, would I be excited about, you know, my very buttoned down PowerPoint with all of that information, like, you know, very detailed, getting handed to one of our competitors?
I would not.
And so I think it was really kind of that, like, that's kind of that gray area where it's not like roadmap and items like that.
But, you know, the way that I characterize it is like, I don't think if somebody were to land on my information about ICP and Persona, like a competitor is going to do a big pivot.
In the same way that if, like, for example, they saw something really crazy in our roadmap, they might.

But nevertheless, I think it's just, you know, wanting to get a comfort for how protected are the inputs that we're going to be providing you in that kind of spectrum of, you know, very, and not at all, right?
Yeah, that makes perfect sense.
I guess, Bridget, for some of our enterprise clients, have we done anything differently around sharing resources with

And then we have each of those account teams has their own logins as well to the different LLMs.
No, nothing is, like, we have all the settings set.
So, like, nothing is learning or being trained on any of your data or anything we upload either.
And then there's no way that that information would be accessible by the clients either.
Like, I'm sure maybe you saw the demo from Tyler and Marcel, but, like, we don't even have actually clients in the product today.
Like, it's all operated by our team, so there would be no way for them to – there would be, like, physical –

basically no way for them to get access to anything, but yeah, if there's anything else that would make you feel better, we can talk about it or put it in the contract, but it definitely has come up in the past and has not been a problem ever before, if that helps.
Yes, it looks like Sarah just joined Sarah for Contacts.

They would not put any of our documents directly into the LLMs.

Obviously, when we're basically having them design content for us, they would be needed to take inspiration from whatever prep materials we give them, but they and only their team is working with the LLMs, so none of their other clients or anything like that would be working with any of the content.
And again, we're not providing stuff like roadmaps or anything like that.
It would be
And, you know, the gray area that James had described before, which is, hey, we're providing information on the persona targets or the ideal customer profiles.

Like, we wouldn't necessarily want to hand that directly to a competitor, but to James' point, a competitor could easily arrive at pretty similar kind of understanding of the market because we're all in the same market addressing the same set of customers.
So I'm not, like, super worried about that as a risk for us.
The bigger risk is falling behind on the content, not ranking in the engines, not getting organic traffic coming to the site.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Finalize the GrowthX \<\> Netwrix contract.](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**AI Use Clarified:** GrowthX uses private, enterprise-licensed LLMs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini) with data isolation protocols to prevent commingling or model training.](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=371.0)
  - [**Contract Finalized:** All open items were resolved, including AI language, IP ownership (Sec 2.1), confidentiality caps (Sec 6.2), and quarterly billing for Phase 2.](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=1542.0)
  - [**Path to Signature:** Sarah Shoults will send the final contract via DocuSign to Marcel (GrowthX) and copy Tyler for visibility.](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=1629.0)
  - [**Kickoff Prep:** Tyler will send a kickoff email with a resource list and schedule the kickoff call immediately after the contract is signed.](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=1766.0)

## Topics

### AI & Data Security

  - [**Netwrix Concern:** Data security, specifically preventing sensitive info (ICPs, personas) from training public LLMs or being commingled with other clients.](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=371.0)
  - [**GrowthX Solution:**](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=433.0)
      - [Uses private, enterprise-licensed LLMs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini).](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=435.0)
      - [Data is isolated: Netwrix documents are for team reference only and are never fed into the LLMs.](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=457.0)
      - [Workflows use only GrowthX-created artifacts, ensuring no commingling.](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=457.0)
      - [Account teams are partitioned to prevent competitive overlap.](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=610.0)
  - [**Contract Language:** The contract will be updated to reflect this, specifying enterprise-licensed tools and excluding public models.](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=826.0)

### IP Ownership (Sec 2.1)

  - [**Resolution:** Agreed to revert Tyler's redline and accept Sarah's latest version.](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=1020.0)
  - [**Rationale:** This maintains a clear distinction: Netwrix owns its pre-existing IP and data, while GrowthX owns its underlying platform. Netwrix owns all final "work product deliverables."](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=1075.0)

### Confidentiality Cap (Sec 6.2)

  - [**Resolution:** Netwrix confirmed acceptance of the 2x cap on breach of confidentiality.](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=1181.0)

### Phase 2 Billing

  - [**Resolution:** Agreed to quarterly billing for Phase 2.](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=1542.0)
  - [**Rationale:** This aligns with the original agreement and reduces administrative overhead for both parties.](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=1542.0)
  - [**Language:** "Billed on the effective date and then subsequently on a quarterly basis."](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=1596.0)

### Logistics & Staffing

  - [**Netwrix Request:** James asked about the account manager's time zone to help staff Netwrix's Europe-based team.](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=1680.0)
  - [**GrowthX Response:** Tyler will request a manager who can accommodate earlier meetings, prioritizing quality over time zone.](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=1680.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Sarah Shoults:**](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=1620.0)
      - [Update contract with final AI and billing language.](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=1234.0)
      - [Send final contract via DocuSign to Marcel (GrowthX), copying Tyler.](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=1629.0)
  - [**Tyler Pavlas:**](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=1766.0)
      - [Send kickoff email with resource list and schedule kickoff call after contract is signed.](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=1766.0)
      - [Confirm account manager assignment and time zone with James.](https://fathom.video/share/B9d3QxCQFbAsk5Y8tsyVk_siYhAdACfY?tab=summary&timestamp=1680.0)

action_items.description: Finalize contract: revert 2.1, fix typo, accept 6.2, set quarterly billing; send clean to Tyler; route DocuSign incl GrowthX signatories
Assign Netwrix account team; notify James; accommodate early-day meetings
